rationale: Re-ran my round-1 falsification method against 59031008 in a fresh /tmp throwaway clone. Fix adds resetPanelCreateFlags(t), called at the top of every t.Run subtest, resetting panelCreateCmd's spec/target/bead/round flag values + Changed to defaults before each Execute() -- closing the cobra flag-leak I found. Verified: (1) with the fix and code intact, go test -run TestPanelCreate_RejectsUnsafeSlugAndControlBytes -count=1 passes 10/10. (2) Deleting rejectControlBytes("--target", target) in panel.go now fails EXACTLY newline_in_--target and C1_CSI_in_--target, all 8 other rows pass. (3) Deleting rejectControlBytes("--bead", beadID) now fails EXACTLY newline_in_--bead and C1_CSI_in_--bead, all others pass. (4) Deleting validatePanelSlug's rejectControlBytes("slug", slug) now fails EXACTLY newline_in_slug and C1_CSI_in_slug, all others pass. Each control-byte row is now cleanly attributable to its own validation call with zero cross-row masking. My round-1 item is fully ADDRESSED; no new issues found in the one-file, +17-line delta.

findings.description: Round-1 finding (cobra --bead flag leak across t.Run subtests in TestPanelCreate_RejectsUnsafeSlugAndControlBytes masking the --target control-byte check) is ADDRESSED by 59031008's resetPanelCreateFlags(t) helper. Confirmed via three independent revert-and-confirm-fail passes (delete --target check / delete --bead check / delete slug check) that each now fails only its own two corresponding subtests.
